notes: Smallest tool-less round-trip pinning the terminal-text end signal for Mistral Vibe: one user prompt, one assistant reply with non-empty content and no tool_calls, asserting the ready→working→ready arc and final ready. The end signal is the terminal role:assistant line with no tool_calls → parser.go maps it to turn_done with AssistantText=TruncateAssistantText(content) (parser.go:67-75), IsAgentDone()=true, classifier rule 2b fires working→ready. BACKFLOW (live basic-turn recording): mistral-vibe is a PRESESSION adapter — a transient proc-<PID> row is born ready BEFORE the real session_<id> transcript dir reconciles under a DIFFERENT session_id; modeled as presession_birth → session_birth(new_session) → pid_bind, every later phase same_session_as session_birth (birth templated from the codex sibling). Offline replay cannot reproduce vibe's meta.json sidecar, so model + tokens are UNVERIFIABLE offline — observations assert agent only (cost_nonzero=false, tokens_nonzero=false), matching kiro-cli. Overlaps intentionally with basic-turn — this cell pins the end-signal mechanism. Final state = ready.
source: .specs/agent-scenarios.md → Feature: Turn end via terminal text

text: proc-<PID> pre-session appears in ready within 1s of launch (PID bound before the transcript dir exists)
Real session_<id> reconciles (different session_id) when the transcript directory appears, born ready
vibe PID bound to the real session (matched by cwd)
User prompt drives ready → working (role:user → user_message) on the real session
Terminal role:assistant line with content and no tool_calls maps to turn_done → state to ready (rule 2b via IsAgentDone); state never entered waiting
